title: Bridge Essay: Social rank modulates methamphetamine-seeking in dominant and subordinate male r

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Wind/Pride** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Wind and pride — ego, narcissism, social dominance, self-esteem, status, social hierarchy in neuroscience, self-enhancement, hubris.

**Social rank modulates methamphetamine-seeking in dominant and subordinate male rodents via distinct dopaminergic pathways.** — Nat Neurosci. PMID: 40355612. DOI: 10.1038/s41593-025-01951-0. Social status has aprofound impact on mental health and propensity towards drug addiction. However, the neural mechanisms underlying the effects of social rank on drug-seeking behavior remain unclear. Here we found that dominant male rodents (based on the tube test) had denser mesocortical dopaminergic projections and were more resistant to methamphetamine (METH)-seeking, whereas subordinates had heightened dopaminergic function in the mesolimbic pathway and were more vulnerable to METH seeking...

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 4 (Wind/Pride). Evaluation: Excellent (score=7.8)
